expectation: Citation graph giant component still >98%, isolated <=12, pointer coverage declining toward 25%
actual: All three predictions confirmed. Zero-maintenance property validated at N=879.
diff: No surprise. Pointer decay steady at 0.032pp/lesson. Citation graph self-maintaining.

findings.citation_graph_self_maintaining: Edges grew 2455->2588 (+133) with zero manual work. Isolated stayed at 10 despite +30 lessons. Each new lesson adds ~4.4 citation edges automatically.
findings.pointer_coverage_declining: Combined pointer coverage 28.4% (was 31.6% at S403). No new pointers added to INDEX.md. Decline rate stable at 0.032pp/lesson.
findings.retrieval_architecture_divergence: Two retrieval systems have opposite scaling: citation graph improves with scale (more edges per node), pointer coverage degrades (fixed index, growing denominator).
findings.practical_implication: At current trajectory, pointer retrieval will be <25% by ~985L. Citation graph remains >98%. The case for citation-graph-primary retrieval strengthens with every lesson.

verdict: REPLICATION CONFIRMED — S416/S420 findings hold at N=879. Zero-maintenance property is robust.
self_application: Successfully located and used citation_retrieval.py --stats without INDEX.md pointer. The tool was findable via orient.py's underused-tools list — validating the adoption pathway.
